domain: Squid chromatophore (cephalopod skin pigment organ): 18-30 radial muscles around central pigment sac; 2025 study reveals 4 INDEPENDENT motor neurons per chromatophore each controlling petal-shaped quadrant; petal-asymmetric color expansion at 125ms timescale.
specific_mechanism: Single pigment sac decomposed into 4 independently-gated petal-quadrants; each quadrant receives separate neural signal; asymmetric color shapes via 4 parallel quadrant-level gates.
llm_application: CHROMATOPHORE-QUAD 4-channel context-gating per attention head: in MHA, split each head's value-dimension d_v into K=4 equal quadrants {v^(1),v^(2),v^(3),v^(4)}. For each quadrant, compute independent gate g^(k) = sigmoid(W_g^(k) x_t) from current token's hidden state. The head output is concat[g^(1) v^(1); g^(2) v^(2); g^(3) v^(3); g^(4) v^(4)]. Strictly more flexible than Gated-Attention (2505.06708) single per-head gate. Differs from (a) Gated Attention (one gate per head), (b) GQA/MQA (key sharing not value-quadrant), (c) SeerAttention (single-level self-distilled gate), (d) PILL (adapter+gate not value-split) by adding K=4 INDEPENDENT per-quadrant context gates per head.
novelty_claim: Gated Attention applies single per-head sigmoid gate. SeerAttention learns gate via self-distillation. GQA shares keys. CHROMATOPHORE-QUAD recombines (a) PER-HEAD VALUE DIM SPLIT into K=4 + (b) PER-QUADRANT INDEPENDENT context-gate. Two known components combined; K=4 quadrant fixity is the new constraint. Not wholly new.
